Strategy Name: ESG, Price Momentum and Stochastic Optimization
Strategy Description: Combines ESG scores and price momentum using a knapsack optimization algorithm to create a portfolio with high returns and improved risk characteristics.
Core Logic: Price momentum contributes to returns, while ESG criteria improve risk characteristics (lower volatility and drawdowns). The knapsack algorithm finds the optimal mix of both.
Entry Conditions: Use a scaled total ESG score and 12-month price momentum (omitting the last month). Apply a knapsack algorithm (solved via simulated annealing) to select stocks that maximize momentum while meeting a high ESG score constraint.
Indicators Used: OWL Analytics ESG data, 12-month price momentum.
Risk Management Rules: Knapsack optimization, weight caps based on top ESG scores, and simulated annealing to avoid overfitted portfolios.
Market Regime Suitability: Partially; consistent performance and reduced drawdowns, but not a complete hedge.
Volatility Considerations: ESG scores significantly influence risk measures; high ESG stocks are less volatile.
Performance Metrics (CAGR, Sharpe Ratio,: CAGR: 17.47%, Sharpe Ratio: 1.29, Max Drawdown: Not stated (but significantly reduced compared to pure momentum)
Full Notes: The strategy is considered 'Very Complex' due to the stochastic optimization required. It outperforms classical momentum on a risk-adjusted basis.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Knapsack problem, Simulated Annealing, Theil's regression, Stochastic optimization.
